table2_feature_categories.Clinical Meaning: Where the patient is now vs their own baseline
Patient-specific deviation, not population reference only
Speed and acceleration of change over time
Whether a danger-direction trend is sustained and post-drug
Temporal link between the drug and the lab change
Strength of the known drug-lab relationship

table5_example_explanations.explanation: Potassium rose from 4.70 mmol/L to 9.40 mmol/L within 14 days of Captopril initiation, consistent with possible hyperkalemia. The patient also has reduced renal function (eGFR 79), older age (74), polypharmacy (5 medications), which raises the risk. Top model drivers: max_in_window (+3.59), slope_30d (-0.61), slope_7d (-0.49).
table5_example_explanations.suggested_action: Review ACE inhibitor/ARB (or potassium-sparing) dose, repeat potassium, and assess renal function and other potassium-raising drugs.

table5_example_explanations.case: Warfarin → Elevated INR / Bleeding risk (patient Case 10)
table5_example_explanations.explanation: INR rose from 1.30 to 3.70 within 10 days of Warfarin initiation, consistent with possible bleeding. The patient also has reduced renal function (eGFR 14), hepatic impairment, older age (79), polypharmacy (6 medications), which raises the risk. Top model drivers: slope_14d (-0.72), acceleration (-0.69), consecutive_abnormal_trend (-0.67).
table5_example_explanations.suggested_action: Review warfarin dose, recheck INR, and assess bleeding risk and interacting medications.

table5_example_explanations.explanation: eGFR declined by 41% (from 53.72 mL/min/1.73m2 to 31.86 mL/min/1.73m2) within 30 days of Metformin (Glucophage) initiation, consistent with possible renal safety. The patient also has reduced renal function (eGFR 32), hepatic impairment, older age (71), which raises the risk. Top model drivers: min_in_window (+5.19), comorbidity_count (+0.81), current_value (+0.71).
table5_example_explanations.suggested_action: Reassess metformin dose/continuation per renal function, and recheck creatinine and eGFR.
